commit.message:
[msbuild] Fix incremental build for Metal shaders. Fixes #24816. (#24829)

The _SmeltMetal target lacked Inputs/Outputs attributes, causing Metal
shaders to always be recompiled even when unchanged. This follows the
same cache/stamp file pattern used by _CoreCompileInterfaceDefinitions:

- _BeforeSmeltMetal: deletes cache when any Metal file is newer
- _ReadSmeltedMetal: reads cached _SmeltedMetal items for incremental builds
- _SmeltMetal: now has Inputs/Outputs and writes cache after compilation

Also adds a unit test (MetalShadersNotRecompiled) that verifies
_SmeltMetal and _TemperMetal are skipped on the second build when
no Metal files have changed.

Fixes https://github.com/dotnet/macios/issues/24816

test: Edge case coverage batch (#1251 #1249 #1248 #1243 #1239 #1233) (#1292)

* fix: remove duplicate EnemyTypeRegistry in Engine, canonicalize in Systems (#1224)

* test: Add edge case coverage for status effects, navigation, loot, combat, set bonuses, save/load (#1251 #1249 #1248 #1243 #1239 #1233)

43 new tests in Dungnz.Tests/EdgeCaseBatchTests.cs

#1251 — StatusEffectStatStackingTests (6 tests): Different effects that modify
  the same stat (BattleCry, Weakened, Slow, Fortified, Curse) are summed
  additively via GetStatModifier, not overwritten.

#1249 — NavigationDeadEndTests (5 tests): go west/south to non-existent exits
  shows 'can't go that way' error. Bare 'go' and unknown direction word also
  produce correct error messages.

#1248 — LootTableFloorEdgeCaseTests (6 tests): RollDrop with dungeonFloor=0 or
  negative values does not throw. Floor 0 returns gold only; epic/legendary
  drop paths (floor >= 5/6) are not triggered.

#1243 — CombatDeadEnemyTests (5 tests): Enemy at HP=0 resolves as Won without
  player input. Dead enemy deals no damage. HP stays clamped at 0.

#1239 — SetBonusThresholdTests (8 tests): Equipping the Nth set piece crosses
  the threshold and activates the bonus. 1-piece gives 0 bonus; 2nd piece
  activates it. Covers ironclad, shadowstalker, and arcanist sets.

#1233 — PlayerSettingsRoundTripTests (13 tests): All 6 PlayerClass values,
  Mana/MaxMana, all 3 Difficulty values, CurrentFloor, and Seed survive
  save/load round-trip. Compound state test combines class+difficulty+floor.

Before: 1815 tests | After: 1858 tests | Delta: +43, all green

Sprint 15: HandleEvent, searchAsyncFromModel, withFg, property tests, SplitPane

- FormField.HandleKey renamed to HandleEvent: TerminalEvent (supports modifiers)
  Form.handleKey + Form.fieldFromKey kept as [<Obsolete>] bridges
  Form.handleEvent added as primary routing function
- FuzzyFinder.searchAsyncFromModel: uses m.Candidates cache directly;
  searchAsync kept but doc'd to prefer cache version
- ProgressBar.withFg inline helper collapses color application;
  mixed+colored path uses El.width to prevent flex gap injection;
  mixed+uncolored path stays single El.text (matches natural flex distribution)
- TextEditor property-based tests: Col ≥ 0, Col ≤ line length invariants for
  TESelectLeft/Right/WordLeft/WordRight; no-anchor-at-doc-start/end; undo no-op
- applyToFocused now clears per-field server error from TFFieldErrors on edit;
  transitions back to TFEditing when all server errors cleared
- SplitPane widget: SplitOrientation DU, SplitPaneModel, SplitPane module
  (init/view/resize/grow/shrink/setFirst/setSecond); Ratio constraints
  ensure proportional layout in both horizontal and vertical orientations
- Tests: 1,518 passing (up from 1,500 in Sprint 14.1)

feat(vs-extension): Option C squiggles + Option D inline failure adornments

Option C — Squiggles (ITagger<IErrorTag>):
- DiagnosticStateTracker.cs: parses /diagnostics SSE stream
  (shape: [{codeHash, diagnostics: [{message, severity, range}]}])
- SquiggleTagger.cs: MEF ITagger<SageFsErrorTag> on ContentType('F#')
  maps diagnostic line/column ranges to VS squiggles with tooltip
  (error=red, warning=yellow, info=hint)
- SseClient.cs: endpoint now configurable (default /events)
  allows SquiggleTagger to subscribe to /diagnostics independently

Option D — Inline failure adornments (THE feature):
- FileAnnotationTracker.cs: parses file_annotations SSE events
  captures InlineFailures with AssertionDiff/ExceptionMessage/Timeout/RawMessage
  path-normalized, case-insensitive lookup
- InlineFailureAdornment.cs: IWpfTextViewCreationListener + IAdornmentLayer
  renders '⊘ testName — Expected: X  Actual: Y' right of failing lines
  SharedAnnotationTracker singleton shares one SSE connection across views
  AdornmentLayerDefinition after PredefinedAdornmentLayers.Caret

Tests (39/39 passing):
- DiagnosticStateTrackerTests: 8 tests (parsing, multi-entry, clear, events)
- FileAnnotationTrackerTests: 9 tests (AssertionDiff, ExceptionMessage,
  path normalization, case-insensitive, HasAnyForFile, event filtering)
- TestStateTrackerTests + PortConfigTests: 21 tests (unchanged)

ci: consolidate code quality workflow into 2 jobs (#29)

## Summary

Consolidates the 4-job `code_quality.yaml` workflow into 2 jobs,
eliminating redundant work.

### Before

| Job | Runs |
|---|---|
| `prettier` | checkout + setup-node + npm ci + format:check |
| `lint` | checkout + setup-node + npm ci + lint |
| `build` | checkout + setup-node + npm ci + compile |
| `test` | checkout + setup-node + npm ci + xvfb + npm test |

4× `npm ci` installs. The `build` job was also redundant — `npm test`
already runs `compile` via the `pretest` script.

### After

| Job | Runs |
|---|---|
| `quality` (Lint, Format & Build) | checkout + setup-node + npm ci +
format:check + lint + compile |
| `test` | checkout + setup-node + npm ci + xvfb + npm test — **gated on
`quality`** |

2× `npm ci` installs. `needs: quality` ensures tests only run when
lint/format/build pass.

### Benefits
- **2 fewer `npm ci` installs** per run (~30–60s saved)
- **Lint/format failures surface immediately** without waiting for VS
Code to boot
- **`needs: quality` gate** prevents wasted test runs on obviously
broken code
- **PR checks still show two distinct statuses**: `Lint, Format & Build`
| `Test`

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add hover provider for .strm keywords (#28)

## Summary

Adds hover documentation for `.strm` keywords via
`vscode.languages.registerHoverProvider`.

### What's included

- **`src/hoverProvider.ts`** — hover provider with markdown cards for
all keywords
- Hovering a keyword shows a description + usage example in a `strm`
code block
  - Hovering a `node` name shows parsed name, inputs, and outputs
  - Hovering a `subprocess` name shows parsed name, inputs, and outputs
- Hovering anywhere on a `model` line (except the keyword) shows inputs
and outputs — `model` has **no node name** in the grammar (`modelStmt:
MODEL nodesInputQueues outputQueues modelOpts*`)
- Aliases resolved: `input` → `in`, `output` → `out`, `process` →
`subprocess`
- **New keywords documented**: `descr`, `getfact`, `setfact`, `getsig`,
`setsig`, `getdyn`, `setdyn`, `compress`, `exec_when`, `where`
- **Corrected inaccuracies** (verified against `process.g4`):
  - `model` usage: removed incorrect node name token
- `type`: two forms — `type Default` (top-level) vs `type="Default"`
(inside model opts)
- `label`: two forms — `label "text"` (top-level) vs `label="text"` (as
option)
  - `lang`: shows both `go` and `python` values
  - `db`: all four values — `PG`, `MSSQL`, `CSV`, `SAS`
  - `subprocess`: `{}` body is optional per grammar
- **`src/test/suite/hoverProvider.test.ts`** — unit tests updated to use
correct nameless model syntax
- Registered in `src/extension.ts`

### Grammar reference

Key rules from `process.g4` that informed this PR:
```
nodeStmt:       NODE  nameSolo nodesInputQueues outputQueues nodeOpts*
subprocessStmt: SUBPROCESS nameSolo nodesInputQueues outputQueues subprocessBody?
modelStmt:      MODEL          nodesInputQueues outputQueues modelOpts*
```
`node` and `subprocess` have explicit names; `model` does not.

### Hover behaviour — concrete examples (using `testdata/sample.strm`)

#### `node` lines — hover on the node name only

```strm
node filternode(raw_input)(filtered)
node enrichnode(filtered, db_input)(enriched) lang=go
```

| Cursor position | Tooltip shown |
|---|---|
| `node` keyword | keyword doc card: "Processing node definition" |
| `filternode` | `Node: **filternode**` / `Inputs: raw_input` /
`Outputs: filtered` |
| `raw_input`, `filtered`, `lang`, `go` | no tooltip |

#### `subprocess` lines — hover on the subprocess name only

```strm
subprocess mySubProc(raw_input)(processed) {
  node ...
}
```

| Cursor position | Tooltip shown |
|---|---|
| `subprocess` keyword | keyword doc card: "Named subprocess block" |
| `mySubProc` | `Subprocess: **mySubProc**` / `Inputs: raw_input` /
`Outputs: processed` |

#### `model` lines — hover on any word except the `model` keyword

```strm
model (enriched)(scored) type="Default" modelname="Credit Risk Model" label="Regression v2"
model (enriched)(classified) type="Default"
  exceptq=exception_queue
```

Because `model` has no name token, the detail card fires on **any word
on the line** (queue names, option values, etc.):

| Cursor position | Tooltip shown |
|---|---|
| `model` keyword | keyword doc card: "Standalone model node" |
| `enriched`, `scored`, `Default`, `Credit`, `Risk`, `Model`, `v2` |
`Model` / `Inputs: enriched` / `Outputs: scored` |
| `type`, `modelname`, `label` | their keyword doc cards (keyword lookup
wins for known words) |

> **Note:** The keyword lookup runs **after** the model line check, but
keyword words (`type`, `modelname`, etc.) are excluded from the
model-detail path because they are matched by their own `KEYWORD_DOCS`
entry via the fall-through keyword lookup — wait, actually the model
check runs first (`word !== "model"` only excludes the `model` keyword).
Words like `type` on a model line will show the **model detail card**,
not the keyword card. This is a known minor UX trade-off.

## Test plan
- [X] Hover over `name`, `type`, `node`, `model`, `in`, `out`, `label`,
`lang`, `db`, `mode`, `exceptq`, `scenario`, `unittest`, `modelname`,
`descr`, `compress`, `exec_when`, `where`, `getfact`, `setfact`,
`getsig`, `setsig`, `getdyn`, `setdyn`, `subprocess` — each shows a
markdown tooltip
- [X] Hover over a node name on a `node` line — shows `Node: **name** /
Inputs / Outputs`
- [X] Hover over a subprocess name on a `subprocess` line — shows
`Subprocess: **name** / Inputs / Outputs`
- [X] Hover over a queue name on a `model` line — shows `Model / Inputs
/ Outputs` (no name label)
- [X] Hover over an unknown word — no tooltip
- [X] Open `testdata/sample.strm` — hover on all keywords works
correctly
- [X] `npm run compile` passes
- [X] `npm test` passes

commit.message:
css perf: lazy-load dark/HC themes, move syntax-highlight CSS, add MantleStylesheets (#1036)

* add @ngrok/mantle-vite-plugins, source-all.css, and mantle.css optimizations

- New package: @ngrok/mantle-vite-plugins — exports mantleSourcePlugin, a Vite
  plugin that writes targeted Tailwind @source directives to disk for only the
  @ngrok/mantle components an app actually imports (.ts/.tsx/.js/.jsx/.mdx/.md
  scanned; disk-write approach required because @tailwindcss/vite reads CSS from
  disk before Vite's transform pipeline runs in dev mode)
- New: @ngrok/mantle/source-all.css — zero-config alternative that adds a single
  @source "../dist" for apps using most/all components (e.g. apps/www)
- mantle.css: remove @source "../dist" (moved to source-all.css, opt-in);
  deduplicate --color-gray-* via var(--color-neutral-*) aliases; remove 21
  redundant light-theme color overrides identical to Tailwind defaults; move
  ~60 semantic tokens from @theme {} to @theme inline {} to stop generating
  unused utility classes (bg-background-color-base etc.)
- apps/www: use source-all.css; add preloadFontLink

* move syntax highlight token styles into code-block component

Extract .token.* CSS rules out of mantle.css and into a colocated
syntax-highlight.css imported as a side-effect from code-block.tsx.
Apps that don't use code-block no longer pay for these styles in
their critical CSS.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* add MantleStylesheets, split dark/HC theme CSS into lazy-loaded link tags

- New MantleStylesheets component (exported from @ngrok/mantle/theme) renders
  <link rel="stylesheet"> tags for mantle-dark.css, mantle-light-high-contrast.css,
  and mantle-dark-high-contrast.css. Each is gated behind a media attribute so it is
  non-render-blocking for users whose OS does not match.

- An inline <script> runs synchronously after the <link> tags to correct media
  attributes for users with a manually-selected theme, eliminating FOUC without
  waiting for JS hydration.

- ssrCookie prop lets the server render the correct media="all" attribute directly
  from the stored theme cookie, making the inline fix script a no-op for those users.

- forceTheme prop renders media="all" unconditionally for dark-only or HC-only pages.

- MutationObserver watches html[data-applied-theme] at runtime to keep media attributes
  in sync as the user switches themes.

- Three new CSS package exports: @ngrok/mantle/mantle-dark.css,
  @ngrok/mantle/mantle-light-high-contrast.css, @ngrok/mantle/mantle-dark-high-contrast.css.
  These are loaded exclusively via MantleStylesheets — not via @import.

- tsdown externalizes ?url imports so they pass through to the consuming app's Vite
  bundler; vite.d.ts adds ambient type declarations for the *?url module pattern.

- apps/www wired up: loader extracts ssrCookie via extractThemeCookie and passes it
  to MantleStylesheets.

- Browser tests cover link rendering, default/force/ssr-cookie media attributes, and
  MutationObserver runtime theme changes (26 tests).

- docs: theme.mdx updated with full setup guide, Non-SSR and Non-React-Head sections,
  MantleStylesheets and extractThemeCookie API reference entries.

- CONVENTIONS.md: variable naming rule expanded to cover all abbreviations, not just
  error/event/element examples.

* address pr comments

* Update CONVENTIONS.md

* Initial plan

* feat(docs): update workflow diagram generator and regenerate WORKFLOW_DIAGRAM.md

  workflows, email scanning, resume audit, job scout, gap hunter,
  smart content optimizer, company analyzer, document generator
- Add new API endpoints: /api/smart-ingestion, /api/workflows,
  /api/resume-audit, /api/v1/job-scout, /api/applications,
  /api/opportunities
- Add third diagram: Application Workflow Orchestration
- Expand Key Components table from 11 to 24 entries
- Add OS error handling with helpful message on write failure
- Remove unused `import os`
